title: SEELE Essenes connection any plausibility in it
display_title: SEELE Essenes connection any plausibility in it
wikitext:
[]: SEELE = Essenes connection, any plausibility in it?             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Sep 12, 2008 9:47 am I've seen this come up time to time in here too. On what exactly this is based as according to CI SEELE was established in Middle Ages?

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Sep 12, 2008 9:52 am Most likely the theory that the Dead Sea Scrolls were the library of the Essenes, perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Sep 12, 2008 9:54 am AyrYntake wrote: Most likely the theory that the Dead Sea Scrolls were the library of the Essenes, perhaps?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 12, 2008 10:20 am http://www.evacommentary.org/op/op_2.html#cut044

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Sep 12, 2008 10:40 am Reichu wrote: http://www.evacommentary.org/op/op_2.html#cut044

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Fri Sep 12, 2008 1:33 pm Wow, that had to be the most interesting thing I read this week.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 13, 2008 11:46 pm Interestingly, I remembered reading something about "ESSENE" on the website I had found that I posted about in the "Project Meeting" thread. I relocated the primary paragraph concerning ESSENE, though I have no idea what it says. This is, however, from the "Screenplay" section, and uses a direct quote from episode 17, but with "ESSENE" in place of "SEELE". Therefore, the change of the organization's name appears to have been done at the last minute. 脚本版 ［設定］ １． ゼーレの登場 ・国連を裏から操る謎の組織ゼーレが登場。人類補完委員会のさらに上位に位置します。また、ゼーレは「エッセネ」と呼ばれていることから死海文書を所有者していたユダヤ教のエッセネ派との関係が推測されます。ゼーレに宗教的な要素が集中したために、ゲンドウからは宗教色はなくなるとともに、二つの補完計画の対立が生まれます。つまり、「原罪」の問題を語るのはゼーレの役目となり、ゲンドウはそれを冷ややかに見つめながら、独自の補完計画（心の補完含む）を進めようとします。 脚本版１７話冬月「エッセネもあわてて行動表を修正しているだろう」 ゲンドウ「死海文書にない事件も起こる。老人にはいい薬だろう」

[]:             Quote Post by pat457 » Sun Sep 14, 2008 7:15 am A bit off-topic: Sadamoto depicted the SDSS as something like a book (codex?) in the manga, didn't he? I don't know if that would apply to anime canon, but then again ?? (monsho) is a more general term than the English 'scrolls'. My Japanese dictionary (???????; Obunsha Kokugo Jiten) defines Monsho as: ???????? "Documents; Records."

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Sep 14, 2008 10:17 am I wish I could read that mess of squiggles, but what you mention certainly makes this one of the more itneresting threads i've seen.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2008 11:41 am NAveryW wrote: Therefore, the change of the organization's name appears to have been done at the last minute.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Sep 14, 2008 12:23 pm Reichu wrote: But, interestingly, episode #13 (that's what it's listed as here) is still called "Seele, Throne of Souls", and "Seele" is directly mentioned in the dialogue.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Sep 14, 2008 12:44 pm The entry plug being "tamashii no za" (throne of a soul) is right there in Rei's monologue, actually. And, no, "Seele" is being used exactly the same. Both of the lines where it appears are, in fact, unchanged between this version of the screenplay and the final show. The appearance in screenplay #17 is probably just a quirk owing to the Essene-->Seele transition, as opposed to any implication that "Essene" is not just a working name for "Seele". (Random: Episode #14 being listed in the book as #13 is a screw-up. Seele bring up the Ireul incident and everything.)
summary:
[]: SEELE = Essenes connection, any plausibility in it?             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Sep 12, 2008 9:47 am I've seen this come up time to time in here too. On what exactly this is based as according to CI SEELE was established in Middle Ages?

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Sep 12, 2008 9:52 am Most likely the theory that the Dead Sea Scrolls were the library
